note: Hostable circuit artifacts for dash-mno-verify, matching the current circuit build. `files` are the small always-hosted artifacts (the members proving key and the wasms), fetched by scripts/fetch_keys.sh. `largeFiles` are the two ~2.3GB proving keys (membership, registration), over the GitHub 2GB asset limit. Host them on object storage or IPFS, fill in each entry's sha256 and a url (or set MNO_KEYS_BASE_URL), and fetch with `scripts/fetch_keys.sh --large`, or rebuild them with scripts/rebuild_proving_keys.sh. PLONK setup is deterministic, so a rebuilt key is byte-identical and its sha256 is stable across builders. The membership and registration wasms changed with the M1 d<n constraint, so the circuit-keys-v2 release must be published with the rebuilt artifacts before fetch_keys.sh can install them, and until then rebuild locally. mno_members is unchanged by M1.

largeFiles.name: mno_membership.zkey
largeFiles.note: ~2.3GB single-tier and registration-verify proving key. Rebuild once with scripts/rebuild_proving_keys.sh, or host it and fill in url + sha256 to fetch with --large.

largeFiles.name: mno_registration.zkey
largeFiles.note: ~2.3GB two-tier registration proving key. Rebuild once with scripts/rebuild_proving_keys.sh, or host it and fill in url + sha256 to fetch with --large.
